scope: Dynamic Model Compensation (DMC) EKF: rule, structural process model, and decision criterion predeclared before evaluation on the higher-fidelity force-model-mismatch slice (truth-side high-order geopotential, luni-solar third body, solar radiation pressure, diurnal-bulge atmospheric-density modulation; estimator keeps the compact two-body+J2+exponential-drag model).
estimator: DMC-EKF -- nine-state Extended Kalman Filter on the augmented state [r, v, w], where w is a first-order Gauss-Markov empirical-acceleration channel (one component per axis) with steady-state standard deviation emp_accel_sigma_mps2 and decorrelation time emp_accel_tau_s. The deterministic flow uses the same compact two-body+J2+drag acceleration as every classical baseline in this study, with w added as an extra acceleration; the estimator absorbs unmodelled accelerations through this structural channel rather than through innovation-driven measurement-noise reweighting.
rationale: Classical statistical OD (Tapley, Schutz, and Born 2004, Section 10; Wright 1981) prescribes a structural dynamic-model-compensation channel as the operational response to dynamics/force-model bias. The compact-model AUKF mechanism diagnostic on Loop 41 motivated this loop's structural counterpart; the predeclared rule below decides positivity at the higher-fidelity force-mismatch slice before any post-hoc inspection.

rule.augmented_state: [r_x, r_y, r_z, v_x, v_y, v_z, w_x, w_y, w_z]
rule.process_model: dr/dt = v, dv/dt = a_compact(r, v) + w, dw/dt = -w / emp_accel_tau_s
rule.decision_predicate: DMC-EKF is a positive contribution if and only if (i) its mean observed-step position RMSE is strictly the lowest among {EKF, UKF, AUKF, PUKF, DMC-EKF} on the higher-fidelity force-mismatch slice, AND (ii) the paired-bootstrap 95% CI for the DMC-EKF-minus-best-non-DMC gap is strictly negative (DMC-EKF strictly better), AND (iii) the gap exceeds the predeclared 3% practical-significance floor of the best-non-DMC mean.
rule.honest_negative: If the predeclared rule does not yield a positive in the sense above, it is reported as a bounded structural-channel negative (no rule retuning is allowed).

thresholds_justification: Empirical-acceleration standard deviation (1e-6 m/s^2) is of the order of drag-class residual accelerations for the LEO regime used here (drag at the canonical altitude band is ~1e-7 to 1e-6 m/s^2). The Gauss-Markov decorrelation time 300 s is a small fraction of the orbital period and matches the operational LEO empirical-acceleration default. The Kalman gating threshold and position/velocity continuous-time process-noise standard deviations match the EKF baseline exactly so the only structural change is the empirical-acceleration channel.

evaluation_protocol.data: Higher-fidelity force-model mismatch slice: truth uses two-body + J2..J6 zonal geopotential + luni-solar third-body acceleration + solar-radiation pressure with cylindrical eclipse + a one-sided diurnal-bulge atmospheric-density modulation; estimators keep the compact two-body+J2+exponential-drag model.
evaluation_protocol.primary_endpoint: Observed-step position RMSE on the same trajectory population as the AUKF mechanism diagnostic.
